dice.subject: expeditions that never returned
dice.aesthetic: silhouette shadow-theater, black cutouts against glowing backdrops
dice.tone: calm and reassuring like late-night radio

concept.name: The Silent Signal
concept.niche: Unsolved expeditions into remote, hostile regions that vanished without trace or contact, exploring the mystery behind their final signals and unexplained disappearances.
concept.tagline: What went silent in the world’s dark corners
concept.audience: Curious adults who love quiet mysteries and real-world anomalies, drawn to stories with no tidy endings.
concept.video_types: 12-minute narrated deep-dives, 3/week; 30s vertical clips extracted from each for social
concept.brief: Each video opens with a sudden, static-filled silence — a radio burst cutting into black — then the narrator calmly explains the expedition, its final transmission, and why no one ever found it. The tone is patient, like a long-ago radio broadcast from a lost station. The mystery is never fully solved, only gently echoed.
concept.style_suffix: Medium: wide, shadow-theater silhouette cutouts. Line quality: soft, slightly blurred edges with subtle grain. Color palette: deep indigo, charcoal gray, blood orange, and electric violet. Lighting: backlit glow from the edges, casting long, dramatic shadows. Composition: centered silhouettes against glowing voids or foggy terrain. Texture: paper-like matte finish with faint static patterns resembling radio noise.
concept.negative_style: real people, facial features, text, on-screen data, motion blur, bright highlights, natural lighting, sunlight, digital effects, readable text, gibberish letters, photorealistic, watermark
concept.music_vibe: Minimal synth pads, distant hums, slow pulse, ambient radio static
concept.voice_instruct: Speak with quiet certainty, like a seasoned listener tuning in after years. Pause slightly between sentences. Let silence breathe between facts.
topic_bank: The 1967 Mount Erebus Expedition and the lost radio log
The 1938 Arctic Survey Team and their final signal in fog
The 1953 Greenland Ice Drilling Mission with no return
The 1987 Chilean Andes Expedition that went silent mid-descent
The 1971 Antarctic Snowmobile Patrol and the missing transmission
The 1943 Pacific Island Search Party and the last message in Morse
The 1960 Tibetan Ridge Expedition and the unexplained silence
The 1992 Himalayan Base Camp team with no distress calls
The 1925 Siberian Forest Patrol and the frozen radio
The 1959 Greenland Ice Shelf Survey and the last burst of static
example_titles: They Vanished. No Signal. No Trace.
The Last Transmission from Mount Erebus
Silence in the Arctic: What Was Lost
A Radio Signal That Never Was
The Expedition That Never Came Home
